title: Ευρωπαϊκές Διασταυρούμενες Αναφορές για την Κυπριακή Νομοθεσία
title_en: EU Cross-Reference Index for Cypriot Law
description: Cross-reference index mapping Cypriot national legislation to EU directives and regulations. Cyprus, as an EU member state since 2004, has transposed all major EU digital, data protection, and cybersecurity directives into national law. This index provides traceability between Cypriot statutes and their EU legal basis.

provisions.title: GDPR Implementation
provisions.content: Cyprus implements the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 through the Law on Personal Data Protection, Law 125(I)/2018 (Ο περί Προστασίας των Φυσικών Προσώπων έναντι της Επεξεργασίας των Δεδομένων Προσωπικού Χαρακτήρα και της Ελεύθερης Κυκλοφορίας των Δεδομένων αυτών Νόμος). This law designates the Commissioner for Personal Data Protection as the national supervisory authority under Article 51 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679. The Commissioner is granted the investigatory, corrective, authorisation, and advisory powers required under Chapter VI of the GDPR. The law also implements the Law Enforcement Directive (EU) 2016/680 through separate provisions governing processing by competent authorities for law enforcement purposes.

provisions.title: NIS2 Directive Transposition
provisions.content: Cyprus transposes Directive (EU) 2022/2555 (NIS2) on the security of network and information systems through the Law on Security of Network and Information Systems. The national transposition repeals and replaces the earlier NIS1 implementation and establishes the Digital Security Authority (Αρχή Ψηφιακής Ασφάλειας) as the national competent authority and single point of contact. The law establishes sector-specific requirements for essential and important entities, risk management obligations, incident reporting timelines (24-hour early warning, 72-hour notification, one-month final report), and supply chain security measures consistent with Article 21 of Directive (EU) 2022/2555.

provisions.title: Electronic Communications Framework
provisions.content: The Electronic Communications Law (Law 112(I)/2004) transposes the EU Electronic Communications Framework, including Directive 2002/21/EC (Framework Directive), Directive 2002/19/EC (Access Directive), Directive 2002/20/EC (Authorisation Directive), and Directive 2002/22/EC (Universal Service Directive). Subsequent amendments implement Directive 2009/136/EC and Directive 2009/140/EC. The law further incorporates the European Electronic Communications Code, Directive (EU) 2018/1972, establishing the Office of the Commissioner of Electronic Communications and Postal Regulations (OCECPR) as the national regulatory authority.

provisions.title: Electronic Commerce Directive Implementation
provisions.content: The Electronic Commerce Law (Law 156(I)/2004) transposes Directive 2000/31/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on electronic commerce (E-Commerce Directive). The law establishes the country-of-origin principle for information society service providers established in Cyprus, sets out the conditions for limited liability of intermediary service providers (mere conduit, caching, hosting), and requires certain information to be provided by electronic commerce service providers. The law has been supplemented by measures implementing Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 (Digital Services Act) obligations applicable to intermediary services.

provisions.title: eIDAS Regulation Implementation
provisions.content: Cyprus implements Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 (eIDAS) on electronic identification and trust services through the Electronic Identification and Trust Services Law. The law designates the Deputy Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digital Policy as the supervisory body for trust service providers and maintains the national trusted list in accordance with Article 22 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The law further prepares for the updated requirements under Regulation (EU) 2024/1183 amending the eIDAS Regulation to establish a European Digital Identity framework, including the forthcoming European Digital Identity Wallet.

provisions.title: Cybercrime Convention and EU Framework
provisions.content: Cyprus ratified the Council of Europe Convention on Cybercrime (Budapest Convention, CETS No. 185) and implements its requirements through Law 22(III)/2004 on Computer Misuse and Related Crimes. Cyprus also implements Council Framework Decision 2005/222/JHA on attacks against information systems, subsequently replaced by Directive 2013/40/EU on attacks against information systems, establishing minimum standards for criminal offences and penalties including unauthorized access, illegal system interference, illegal data interference, and interception of computer data.

provisions.title: Critical Infrastructure Protection
provisions.content: Cyprus implements Council Directive 2008/114/EC on the identification and designation of European critical infrastructures through its Critical Infrastructure Protection Law. The Directive (EU) 2022/2557 on the resilience of critical entities (CER Directive), which repeals Directive 2008/114/EC, is being transposed through updated national measures. The national framework designates competent authorities for each critical sector, establishes a risk assessment process, and requires operators of critical infrastructure to develop and implement security plans addressing physical and cyber threats.

provisions.title: Trade Secrets Directive
provisions.content: Cyprus implements Directive (EU) 2016/943 on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure through the Protection of Confidential Information Law. The Directive harmonises the definition of trade secrets, the conditions under which acquisition, use, and disclosure of trade secrets are considered unlawful, and the available civil remedies. Cyprus national law provides for injunctive measures, corrective measures, and damages, as required by Articles 10-14 of Directive (EU) 2016/943.

provisions.title: Telecom Privacy Directive
provisions.content: The Processing of Personal Data (Telecommunications) Law (Law 92(I)/2007) transposes Directive 2002/58/EC (ePrivacy Directive) concerning the processing of personal data and the protection of privacy in the electronic communications sector. The law governs confidentiality of communications, traffic data, location data, unsolicited communications (spam), and the use of cookies and similar tracking technologies consistent with Article 5(3) of Directive 2002/58/EC as amended by Directive 2009/136/EC.

provisions.title: Digital Government and e-Government Directive
provisions.content: The Electronic Government Law transposes relevant EU measures on digital public services including Directive (EU) 2019/1024 on open data and the re-use of public sector information (Open Data Directive, amending Directive 2003/98/EC). The law also implements the Single Digital Gateway Regulation (EU) 2018/1724, requiring Cyprus to make administrative procedures available online through the 'Your Europe' portal. The Deputy Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digital Policy is designated as the national coordinator for implementing the once-only principle under Regulation (EU) 2018/1724.

definitions.definition: Cyprus has been a member of the European Union since 1 May 2004, and is therefore fully subject to EU law including regulations that are directly applicable and directives that require national transposition.

The process by which Cyprus enacts national legislation to implement the binding objectives of EU directives within the timeframe specified by each directive, while adapting the measures to the national legal system.
